channel.title: Add WP REST API url directly from command line when "frontity create"
channel.description:
# Context
Our users are getting a difficult step to solve when they create their first project with frontity. It is not clear the URL of the WP REST API that should be set in the `frontity.settings.js` under the `wp-source` package configuration
- https://github.com/frontity/docs/issues/146 - https://github.com/frontity/docs/issues/102
There are also some community threads regarding this: - https://community.frontity.org/t/error-add-the-url-of-your-wordpress-rest-api-in-state-source-api/1370 - https://community.frontity.org/t/error-fetching-from-wordpress/2146/12
The DevRel team will improve the docs but we could also...
# Description
Setting the `state.source.api` value from the command line to improve the developer experience when creating Frontity projects
# Possible solution
When we do `npx frontity create my-frontity-project` one of the questions that the CLI could ask could be:
``` (wpUrl) → What is the URL of the homepage of your Wordpress site (default=https://test.frontity.io/) ? (isWpCom) → Is it a Wordpress.com site (y/N)? ```
### Use Case 1
- `isWpCom`=**`no`** - `wpUrl`=**`https://test.frontity.io/`**
→ `state.source.api` = **`https://test.frontity.io/wp-json/`**
### Use Case 2
- `wpUrl`=**`https://juanmaguitarblog.wordpress.com/`** - `isWpCom`=**`yes`** _(in this case this value could be automatically detected and avoid the second question)_
`state.source.api` = **`https://wordpress.com/posts/juanmaguitarblog.wordpress.com/wp-json/`**
### Use Case 3
- `wpUrl`=**`https://instajuegos.home.blog/ `** - `isWpCom`=**`yes`**
_(This specific combination of [`home.blog`](https://wordpress.com/blog/2018/11/28/announcing-free-dotblog-subdomains/) + `isWpCom=yes` will result in a wordpress.com subdomain format of REST API)_
`state.source.api` = **`https://public-api.wordpress.com/wp/v2/sites/instajuegos.home.blog/posts/`**
### Use Case 4
- `wpUrl`=**`https://mycoolwpsite.com `** - `isWpCom`=**`yes`** (Paid plan w/ custom domain: Personal, Business...)
`state.source.api` = **`https://mycoolwpsite.com/wp-json/`**
### Use Case 5
- `wpUrl`=**`https://mycoolwpsite.wpcomstaging.com`** - `isWpCom`=**`yes`** (Business plan w/ plugins installed w/o custom domain)
`state.source.api` = **`https://mycoolwpsite.wpcomstaging.com/wp-json/`**
### Use Case 6
- `wpUrl`=**`https://localhost:4000`** - `isWpCom`=**`no`**
`state.source.api` = **`http://localhost:4000/wp-json`**

item.title: Add WP REST API url directly from command line when "frontity create"
item.dc:creator: mburridge
item.description:
juanma:

There’s already a --no-prompt option, isnt’ there? → https://docs.frontity.org/frontity-cli/create

Maybe I should read the docs a bit more carefully!

Read full topic
item.source: Add WP REST API url directly from command line when "frontity create"

item.title: Add WP REST API url directly from command line when "frontity create"
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
mburridge:

Could we also avoid the prompt by adding a command line switch such as npx frontity create my-frontity-project -a https://test.frontity.io/wp-json/

I like it

mburridge:

How about also a -noprompt switch which will default to the mars-theme and reply N to the email signup question?

There’s already a --no-prompt option, isnt’ there? → https://docs.frontity.org/frontity-cli/create

Read full topic
item.source: Add WP REST API url directly from command line when "frontity create"

item.title: Add WP REST API url directly from command line when "frontity create"
item.dc:creator: mburridge
item.description:
This is a very good idea. Could we also avoid the prompt by adding a command line switch such as npx frontity create my-frontity-project -a https://test.frontity.io/wp-json/?

How about also a -noprompt switch which will default to the mars-theme and reply N to the email signup question?

Read full topic
item.source: Add WP REST API url directly from command line when "frontity create"

item.title: Add WP REST API url directly from command line when "frontity create"
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Context

Our users are getting a difficult step to solve when they create their first project with frontity. It is not clear the URL of the WP REST API that should be set in the frontity.settings.js under the wp-source package configuration

  • https://github.com/frontity/docs/issues/146
  • https://github.com/frontity/docs/issues/102

There are also some community threads regarding this:

  • Error: Add the URL of your WordPress REST API in state.source.api
  • Error fetching from Wordpress

The DevRel team will improve the docs but we could also…

Description

Setting the state.source.api value from the command line to improve the developer experience when creating Frontity projects

Possible solution

When we do npx frontity create my-frontity-project one of the questions that the CLI could ask could be:

(wpUrl) → What is the URL of the homepage of your Wordpress site (default=https://test.frontity.io/) ?
(isWpCom) → Is it a Wordpress.com site  (y/N)?

Use Case 1

  • isWpCom=no
  • wpUrl=https://test.frontity.io/

→ state.source.api = https://test.frontity.io/wp-json/

Use Case 2

  • wpUrl=https://juanmaguitarblog.wordpress.com/
  • isWpCom=yes (in this case this value could be automatically detected and avoid the second question)

state.source.api = https://wordpress.com/posts/juanmaguitarblog.wordpress.com/wp-json/

Use Case 3

  • wpUrl=https://instajuegos.home.blog/
  • isWpCom=yes

(This specific combination of home.blog + isWpCom=yes will result in a wordpress.com subdomain format of REST API)

state.source.api = https://public-api.wordpress.com/wp/v2/sites/instajuegos.home.blog/posts/

Use Case 4

  • wpUrl=https://mycoolwpsite.com
  • isWpCom=yes (Paid plan w/ custom domain: Personal, Business…)

state.source.api = https://mycoolwpsite.com/wp-json/

Use Case 5

  • wpUrl=https://mycoolwpsite.wpcomstaging.com
  • isWpCom=yes (Business plan w/ plugins installed w/o custom domain)

state.source.api = https://mycoolwpsite.wpcomstaging.com/wp-json/

Use Case 6

  • wpUrl=https://localhost:4000
  • isWpCom=no

state.source.api = http://localhost:4000/wp-json

Read full topic
item.source: Add WP REST API url directly from command line when "frontity create"
